title: Sincerity does not override autonomy
source_observation: Udayakumaran's desire may be emotionally genuine but Manimekalai's choice remains independent

title: Prince is not a one-dimensional villain
source_observation: The character has desire status conflict and unrealised royal responsibility
risk: Reducing him to evil removes tragedy
decision: Show charisma and contradiction without excusing harm
rationale: Preserves tragic complexity without excusing coercion

title: Royal access changes consequences
source_observation: Power affects ability to approach and influence others
risk: Ordinary romance grammar would minimise risk
rationale: Makes the ethical consequences of status visible

title: Previous births do not create ownership
source_observation: Karmic connection exists within epic logic
rationale: Protects present-life autonomy within the epic's rebirth framework

title: Death is multi-causal tragedy
source_observation: Kanchanan acts under mistaken perception after a chain of choices
risk: Simple revenge or punishment reading is reductive
rationale: Assigns responsibility without flattening the causal chain

title: Do not aestheticise fatal violence
source_observation: Death drives later grief and harm
risk: Romantic death imagery could validate pursuit
rationale: Keeps attention on harm consequence and loss

title: Do not invent heroic achievements
decision: Show possibility rather than fabricated greatness
rationale: Prevents invented merit from functioning as retrospective excuse
